title: City Signal Grid: Intersection Regime Clustering
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; the true regime labels and hidden state stay in the evaluator process
strategies: null clustering (all intersections in one group) -> ARI 0 -> normalized ~0.1 on every city
plain Lloyd k-means on raw features: recovers compact isotropic regimes but is distorted by shear (anisotropic cities) and dominated by large-scale nuisance channels (scaled cities)

robust-standardize (median/MAD) + PCA whitening + k-means++ multi-restart: neutralizes scale/nuisance channels and decorrelates sheared regimes -> high ARI across the family, still imperfect on overlapping / many-regime / high-noise cities (headroom)
structure-adaptive methods (GMM/EM for anisotropic covariance, spectral / kNN-graph clustering for non-convex regimes, model-selection over k) that specialize per city geometry
